data.title: Trump-Backed Asfura Declared Honduras Election Winner After Prolonged Count
data.description:
After a prolonged and contentious vote count, Honduras's electoral authority said Nasry “Tito” Asfura has won the Nov. 30 presidential election, edging out television personality Salvador Nasralla in one of the tightest races the country has seen in years. The authority's published tally showed Asfura with 40.3% of the vote versus 39.5% for Nasralla, with ruling-party candidate Rixi Moncada trailing at 19.2%. The result came more than three weeks after election day, following repeated allegations of irregularities from Nasralla and Moncada, and was formally released after markets closed on Christmas Eve, a timing that may have limited the immediate risk of unrest.

Nasralla rejected the outcome in a livestream on Facebook (META, Financial) and TikTok, calling the vote a fraud and saying results were declared without all ballots being counted, while also alleging manipulation in congressional and mayoral races. He compared the situation to Venezuela's disputed election last year under Nicolas Maduro and referenced Honduras's own 2017 election, which triggered deadly protests after a result widely viewed as fraudulent. Even so, Nasralla said he would pursue peaceful and legal avenues rather than mobilizing supporters in the streets, framing his response as an effort to “defend” the vote without escalating tensions.

From an investor perspective, early market signals suggested cautious optimism. Honduran bonds rallied after Asfura, a civil engineer and former two-term mayor of Tegucigalpa, took the lead in preliminary counts, reflecting expectations of a more market-friendly agenda. Asfura has pledged to cut red tape, seek new free trade agreements, and promote large infrastructure projects, including a liquefied gas pipeline linking Mexico, El Salvador, and Honduras, alongside fiscal incentives aimed at attracting foreign direct investment. He has also said he would reverse Honduras's 2023 decision under President Xiomara Castro to establish diplomatic ties with Beijing, instead prioritizing closer relations with the US, Taiwan, and Israel. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio was quick to congratulate Asfura, a development that could align with support he received from Donald Trump in the days leading up to the vote, even as Honduras's long history of electoral controversy continues to shape the political and investment backdrop.

data.title: WisdomTree Europe Quality Dividend Growth Fund (EUDG) Announces Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
lang="en">

WisdomTree Europe Quality Dividend Growth Fund ETF Analysis

Key Takeaways

  • WisdomTree Europe Quality Dividend Growth Fund ETF (EUDG, Financial) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.1973 per share.
  • The ETF exhibits strong financial health with a operating margin of 19.04% and a net margin of 12.74%.
  • Current market sentiment is positive, with a year-to-date price change of 26.69%.

Dividend Announcement

WisdomTree Europe Quality Dividend Growth Fund ETF (EUDG) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.1973 per share. Shareholders on record by December 26 will receive the payment on December 30. The ex-dividend date is also December 26. Investors can explore further details on the fund's dividend performance through its Dividend Scorecard and Yield Chart.

Company Overview

The WisdomTree Europe Quality Dividend Growth Fund ETF, trading under the symbol EUDG, is a fund designed to provide investors with exposure to high-quality dividend-paying companies in Europe. The ETF is listed on the ARCA exchange and has a market capitalization of $86.8 million. While specific sector and industry details are not provided, the fund's focus on dividend growth suggests a strategy centered around stable, mature companies with strong cash flows.

Financial Health Analysis

Despite the absence of direct revenue figures, the fund's financial health can be assessed through its profitability metrics. The operating margin stands at 19.04%, while the net margin is 12.74%. These figures indicate efficient management and cost control. The gross margin of 52.01% further underscores the fund's ability to maintain profitability.

On the balance sheet front, the fund's current ratio of 1.35 and debt-to-equity ratio of 1.07 suggest a balanced approach to liquidity and leverage. The Altman Z-Score of 4.29 indicates a low risk of financial distress.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

The fund's valuation metrics reveal a P/E ratio of 44.57, which is relatively high compared to its historical median of 18.7. The P/S ratio is 3.49, aligning closely with its historical median of 3.36. The P/B ratio of 0.8 suggests the fund is trading below its book value, potentially indicating undervaluation.

Technical indicators such as the RSI of 68.9 and moving averages (SMA-20 at 36.89, SMA-50 at 36.15, and SMA-200 at 34.72) reflect a bullish sentiment. Institutional and insider ownership data are not available, but the absence of insider trading activity suggests stability.

Risk Assessment

The fund's financial health grades are robust, with a growth rank of 7 and a profitability rank of 8. Sector-specific risks are not detailed, but the fund's low beta of 0.62 suggests lower volatility compared to the broader market.

While no specific upcoming catalysts are mentioned, the fund's consistent dividend growth and strong financial metrics position it well for future stability.

data.title: Coupang Addresses Data Breach Involving Former Employee
data.description:
South Korean e-commerce giant Coupang recently confirmed a data breach involving a former employee who accessed customer data. The company stated that the breach affected approximately 3,000 customers, but no sensitive information like payment details or login credentials was compromised.

The former employee used a stolen security key to access basic customer information from around 33 million accounts, but only retained data from about 3,000 accounts. After media reports surfaced, the individual deleted all stored information.

Coupang's internal investigation found no evidence of data being shared or transferred to third parties. The company assured that all devices used to access and store customer information, including hard drives, are secure. Accessed information included names, email addresses, phone numbers, and home addresses.

data.title: Palantir Stock Jumped on Wedbush Call, AI Platform Seen as Game-Changer
data.description:
Palantir (PLTR, Financial) shares climbed about 4% last week after Wedbush included the company in its top tech predictions for 2026, citing Palantir's commercial AI momentum and new platform rollouts.

Wedbush said Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) could drive stronger commercial adoption next year and that the company may emerge as “one of the leaders” in the AI software push. The firm suggested Palantir could move toward a much larger valuation over the next two to three years.

Analysts and investors will likely watch AIP adoption, enterprise contract trends and revenue conversion from AI projects before repricing the stock. Wedbush's note frames 2026 as a potential inflection year if Palantir translates platform wins into sustained commercial growth. Seeking Alpha

Traders said the Wedbush bulletin helped lift sentiment after a busy earnings season for AI-related names.

Is PLTR Stock a Buy?

Based on the one year price targets offered by 22 analysts, the average target price for Palantir Technologies Inc is $185.80 with a high estimate of $255.00 and a low estimate of $50.00. The average target implies a downside of -4.29% from the current price of $194.13.

Based on the consensus recommendation from 26 brokerage firms, Palantir Technologies Inc's (PLTR, Financial) average brokerage recommendation is currently 2.8, indicating a "Hold" status. The rating scale ranges from 1 to 5, where 1 signifies Strong Buy, and 5 denotes Sell.

data.title: Sanofi (SNY) to Acquire Dynavax (DVAX) for $2.2 Billion
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Sanofi's acquisition of Dynavax Technologies is valued at approximately $2.2 billion, offering a 39% premium over the closing price.
  • Dynavax's HEPLISAV-B vaccine and shingles candidate Z-1018 will enhance Sanofi's vaccine portfolio.
  • The transaction is expected to conclude in the first quarter of 2026, pending a tender offer for all outstanding shares.

Sanofi (SNY) announced plans to acquire Dynavax Technologies (DVAX, Financial) for $15.50 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately $2.2 billion. This offer represents a 39% premium over Dynavax's closing price on December 23. The acquisition will add Dynavax's HEPLISAV-B adult hepatitis B vaccine and its phase 1/2 shingles candidate Z-1018 to Sanofi's portfolio. Sanofi will initiate a tender offer for all outstanding shares of Dynavax, with the transaction expected to conclude in the first quarter of 2026.

Dynavax Technologies Corp is a biopharmaceutical company focused on leveraging the power of the body's innate and adaptive immune responses through toll-like receptor stimulation. The company operates in the healthcare sector, specifically within the drug manufacturers industry. With a market capitalization of approximately $1.3 billion, Dynavax is positioned as a significant player in the development and commercialization of novel vaccines. The company's primary revenue drivers include its CpG 1018 product and the HEPLISAV-B vaccine.

Financial Health Analysis

Dynavax's financial performance presents a mixed picture:

  • Revenue Growth: The company reported trailing twelve-month revenues of $330.51 million, with a 3-year revenue growth rate of -14.3%, indicating a decline in sales over the period.
  • Profitability: The operating margin stands at 6.48%, while the net margin is -13.13%, reflecting challenges in achieving profitability.
  • Balance Sheet Strength: The company maintains a robust current ratio of 7.62 and a quick ratio of 6.94, indicating strong liquidity. The debt-to-equity ratio is 0.53, suggesting moderate leverage.
  • Warning Signs: The Altman Z-Score of 1.57 places the company in the distress zone, implying a potential risk of bankruptcy within the next two years.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

Dynavax's valuation metrics provide insights into its market positioning:

  • Valuation Ratios: The P/S ratio is 4.5, and the P/B ratio is 2.45, both close to their respective historical lows, indicating potential undervaluation.
  • Analyst Targets: The target price is set at $20.75, with a recommendation score of 2.3, suggesting a moderate buy.
  • Technical Indicators: The RSI (14) is 57.86, indicating a neutral market sentiment, while the stock's volatility is 28.46, reflecting significant price fluctuations.
  • Ownership: Institutional ownership is high at 97.44%, with insider ownership at 1.87%, suggesting strong institutional confidence.

Risk Assessment

Investors should consider the following risks associated with Dynavax:

  • Financial Health Grades: The company's Beneish M-Score of -2.75 indicates it is unlikely to be a manipulator, providing some reassurance regarding financial reporting integrity.
  • Sector-Specific Risks: Operating within the biopharmaceutical sector, Dynavax faces inherent risks related to drug development and regulatory approvals.
  • Volatility and Beta: With a beta of 0.49, the stock exhibits lower volatility compared to the broader market, which may appeal to risk-averse investors.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Takes a Breather -- Why Bulls Might Be Eyeing the Next Upswing
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) still looks attractively priced, despite a strong run this year, top Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon said, keeping an Outperform rating and a $275 price target. He added that, given current levels, “we would be buyers here.”

Rasgon noted Nvidia stock is up roughly 30% year to date but has stalled since July. That pause pushed forward valuation down to just under 25 times earnings, after a roughly 27% multiple contraction this year. By one measure, the stock sits in the bottom 11% of its historical valuation range.

The analyst pointed out Nvidia now trades at about a 13% discount to the broader semiconductor index, a rare relative cheapness that has appeared only a handful of days over the past decade.

Rasgon acknowledged investor worries about a slowdown in AI spending, longer-term capex plans and competition from custom chips. He said those concerns may be overstated and that demand for GPUs remains healthy.

Upcoming product catalysts such as Rubin and the longer-term Blackwell opportunity could refocus attention, Rasgon added. Events like CES and GTC may also help reset expectations for Nvidia's roadmap and address questions around China sales.

data.title: Elon Musk's Fortune Passes $700 Billion
data.description:
Elon Musk just added another milestone to his list his net worth has topped $700 billion. The Tesla (TSLA, Financials) CEO's fortune jumped after the Delaware Supreme Court reinstated his long-disputed 2018 pay package, valued at roughly $139 billion.

The move pushed Musk's total wealth to around $749 billion, according to Forbes, making him the richest person in the world by an even wider margin.

The ruling overturned a 2024 decision that had voided the deal as “excessive.” The state's high court said the earlier ruling went too far, adding that Tesla shareholders had approved the compensation and that Musk hadn't been paid for six years while building the company's growth story.

Musk's wealth has been on a rocket-like trajectory in recent months. SpaceX's rising valuation ahead of a potential 2026 IPO and Tesla's growing push into AI and robotics have fueled investor excitement.

In November, shareholders also reaffirmed their support for Musk's record-breaking $1 trillion incentive plan. Analysts are split on Tesla as electric vehicle demand drops and competition rises.

data.title: Micron Rated Top Large Cap Pick as Analyst Projects Nearly 100% Upside
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU, Financials) posted good first quarter earnings and issued a bullish AI demand estimate, earning Wall Street acclaim. Tristan Gerra of Baird raised Micron's price objective from $235 to $443 and maintained a Buy rating, calling it one of his “top large-cap investment ideas.” New objective offers almost 100% upside from present levels.

Micron's adjusted profits of $4.78 per share surpassed $3.96 and sales rose 56.6% to $13.64 billion, above $12.91 billion projections. Shares rose almost 11% premarket following the news.

Gerra noted rising AI-related bit demand, robust memory and storage fundamentals, and Micron's leadership in high-margin LPDDR5 DRAM. He emphasized that Micron satisfies barely two-thirds of worldwide demand, its tightest supply balance ever.

GPU and accelerator demand could boost Micron's profitability to $42 per share by fiscal 2027, according to the analyst. He said that the company's $20 billion capital expenditure target for FY26 is conservative and within its long-term investment range.

The average price objective of $309.18 represents 20% upside, while the stock has risen almost 200% in 2025.

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Fined €98.6 Million for Alleged Market Abuse in Italy
data.description:
The Italian Competition Authority has imposed a €98.6 million fine on Apple (AAPL, Financial) and its two subsidiaries for allegedly abusing its market dominance when distributing applications to iOS users. The authority claims that Apple has potentially violated European regulations, as its App Store holds a dominant position in providing a platform for developers.
